articles.title: ICE Agent Charged With Assault in Rare State Prosecution
articles.summary: Minnesota prosecutors have spent weeks investigating the conduct of immigration agents who took part in an immigration crackdown in the Twin Cities.

articles.title: An Israeli and a Palestinian activist share a vision for peace in Gaza
articles.summary: Maoz Inon's parents were killed by Hamas in the Oct. 7 attacks. Aziz Abu Sarah's brother died after being tortured in an Israeli military prison. Their new book is The Future Is Peace.

articles.title: Boiling milk and worrying about the Iran war: A New Year dawns in Sri Lanka
articles.summary: In Sri Lanka, Buddhists and Hindus marked their New Year on Tuesday while a war thousands of miles away is making itself felt.

articles.title: ICE Has Detained Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, an 85-Year-Old Widow
articles.summary: After Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé’s husband died, an inheritance battle exploded. Her stepson then used his influence to have her arrested, an Alabama probate judge said.

articles.title: A complex set of negotiations to end Israel's overlapping wars
articles.summary: Israel has agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon, part of a complex web of Mideast negotiations, from Iran to Gaza.

articles.title: Pope Leo takes aim at  'handful of tyrants' spending billions on war amid tensions with Trump
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV condemns "tyrants" fueling war with billions. His calls for global peace during his Africa trip come amid rising tensions with President Trump.

articles.title: RFK Jr. to Reform Health Panel That Determines Which Screenings Insurers Cover
articles.summary: The health secretary said he would reform the panel, which determines whether insurance will cover preventive services like mammograms and colonoscopies.

articles.title: White House Shrugs Off Shaky Economy as War Exceeds Trump’s Timeline
articles.summary: Stocks may be soaring again, but the war in Iran has started to pinch the finances of many Americans.

articles.title: RFK Jr. defends his health agenda and Trump's proposed budget cuts in hearing
articles.summary: In his first appearance this year, the health secretary is taking questions on his record on health, including his controversial moves on vaccines.

articles.title: Commission of Fine Arts votes to move ahead with Trump's proposed victory arch
articles.summary: The seven-member body, whose ranks are composed of Trump appointees, was expected to approve the arch. The proposed monument strongly resembles Paris' Arc de Triomphe, but almost 100 feet taller.

articles.title: Mounting Troubles at Ohio State University Lead to Frustration
articles.summary: Ohio State isn’t the only university in turmoil, but few others have faced so many issues lately. One lawmaker called the school “a national embarrassment.”

articles.title: NPR receives $113 million in charitable gifts
articles.summary: "My hope is that this commitment provides the stability and the spark NPR needs to innovate boldly and strengthen its national network," says Connie Ballmer, who gave $80 million of the $113 million.

articles.title: The Costa Rican Mountain Town Offering Sanctuary to Families Deported by Trump
articles.summary: In a cloud forest village, a network of residents, foreigners and pacifist Quakers offered a precarious yet vital sanctuary for families expelled by the U.S. government.

articles.title: A Polymarket trader made $300,000 betting on Biden's pardons, a new analysis shows
articles.summary: In the final hours of President Biden's term, an anonymous prediction market trader placed lucrative bets on who would be pardoned even as the odds were nearly zero.

articles.title: Iran War timeline continues to shift. And, jury rules Live Nation acted as monopoly
articles.summary: Trump continues to try to declare victory in Iran as the timeline for the war's end shifts. And, a jury determined that Live Nation acted as a monopoly and overcharged ticket buyers.

articles.title: Israel agrees to a ceasefire in its war with Hezbollah in Lebanon, Trump says
articles.summary: The leaders of Israel and Lebanon have agreed on a 10-day ceasefire in Israel's fight against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon, according to President Trump.

articles.title: Indonesia's capital of the future faces doubts in the present
articles.summary: Indonesia is racing to build a new capital, promising a greener, futuristic city. But many citizens aren't convinced it will live up to the vision.

articles.title: Jesus Memes, Threats and a War in Iran: A Portrait of Trump Under Pressure
articles.summary: President Trump has lashed out at enemies, allies and even the pope, and made it harder for Republicans to keep the focus on economic issues in a midterm election year.

articles.title: 3 things to know about naval blockades as U.S. patrols the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The White House says it wants to choke off Iran's oil export revenue. But experts say that blockades are often unpredictable and difficult to enforce.

articles.title: Hungarian Americans say Orbán's defeat could provide roadmap for countering Trump
articles.summary: Hungarian Americans say Viktor Orbán's defeat in Hungary provides a blueprint for countering President Trump's attempts to tilt the electoral playing field ahead of the midterms.

articles.title: Yale Report Finds Colleges Deserve Blame for Higher Education’s Problems
articles.summary: A 10-member committee offered a brutal assessment of academia’s role in creating the forces challenging American colleges and universities.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
